summary: Temporarily disable rust-diffforge terminal telemetry JSONL file writes behind a native boolean toggle.

review_items.summary: Terminal startup, close, app-close, and frontend telemetry events remain callable but no longer create or append to the local terminal telemetry JSONL file while the toggle is false.
Tauri command names, terminal pane lifecycle behavior, auth, billing, hosted API, and database contracts are unchanged.

rules: Terminal telemetry file writes must be disabled by default for now.
A single native boolean must be available to re-enable the existing telemetry writer later.
Disabling telemetry must not change terminal startup, resize, write, close, close-all, or app-close cleanup behavior.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, auth, billing, or hosted API behavior changes are introduced.

patches.summary: Add the native terminal telemetry logging enable/disable boolean.
Make the shared terminal telemetry writer return before creating directories or opening the JSONL file when logging is disabled.

compatibility.notes: The change is local to rust-diffforge diagnostics storage. Existing commands continue to return success without writing telemetry while disabled.

tests.summary: Validate the Rust Tauri host code.
